Comment_Text: No, not everyone loves it. The imperative is that the number of teams qualifying from the groups divides into 2 all the way to the final. 48 teams necessitates the ridiculous situation where 2/3 third place teams qualify. That creates an unfairness going into the knockout stage, where you see 1st v 3rd, 2nd v 2nd, or 1st v 2nd. In a 32 team tournament the advantage came in finishing first in the group where you would play a second placed team. That is across the whole R16.
Label: Substantive / Analytical
Notes: Provides a detailed mathematical and structural breakdown of how the format creates competitive imbalances in the knockout bracket.
